generatedSentence: Jan, a boyish warden of forgotten tech, maintained a meticulous inventory in his housing holder, his mindful gaze scanning sketches of bizarre aliens. He kept an earpiece constantly adjusting, trying to decipher unclear information from the twilight zones outside, a flicker of ecstasy when he thought he heard vocals. His past as a hijacker seeking liberty was now an unacknowledged void, leaving him with only a loppy, ghostly nickname – 'The Finisher'. He’d picked up warts from his time in the warzone, but now sought a romantic calm, hoping to upcycle his skills. A new threat, implacable and urgent, necessitated a revamp of his tabletop strategy, using salvaged jackhammers and a jammer. He learned of underhanded nepotism funding a kidnapping plot involving a radionuclide being developed in a hidden pit, disguised with a hokey brownie stand. The target: an immaculate nuns' sacrament held in a tiffany-style chapel. The conspirators, a group of oily, abusive enemies, were ranked by their vengeful leader who had an elaborate weapon collection, including frog legs fashioned into a grotesque holder. Jan, now unmuzzled and fueled by a surge of hormone, hunkered down, adjusting his bicycle's rotary drive for speed. He knew the wilderness around the facility was a dangerous pit, filled with icicles and the metaphorical claws of surveillance. His perspective was clear: he'd infiltrate through the rectal entrances, a vulnerability he’d rekeyed using lambdas and hyphens from their obscure language. He carried a realistic thermometer as a diversion, alongside his milked eggplants for a nutrient boost, ignoring the mocking heckler on his comms, a notorious lickspittle named Jan himself, creating an ironic double-take, their lifespan now linked to this mission. He found the dormant radionuclide just as the facility’s housing ruptured, a result of his diversionary tactics. An ignorant hulk of a guard, smelling faintly of jasmine and stale whisky, tried to stop him, but Jan, ejecting his parachute from a quick escape harness, left the guard looking naughty and wasted. The finisher untangled the final security protocols, then managed an upsell of their flawed tech to a rival corporation. He thought about the vanity of it all, grabbing a pair of khakis and marveling at the excelsior of freedom, the freckles on his face the only sign of the battle, having finally established new norms for the corrupted institution.
panelSentences: Jan, a boyish warden of forgotten tech, maintained a meticulous inventory in his housing holder, his mindful gaze scanning sketches of bizarre aliens. He kept an earpiece constantly adjusting, trying to decipher unclear information from the twilight zones outside, a flicker of ecstasy when he thought he heard vocals. His past as a hijacker seeking liberty was now an unacknowledged void, leaving him with only a loppy, ghostly nickname – 'The Finisher'. He’d picked up warts from his time in the warzone, but now sought a romantic calm, hoping to upcycle his skills.

A new threat, implacable and urgent, necessitated a revamp of his tabletop strategy, using salvaged jackhammers and a jammer. He learned of underhanded nepotism funding a kidnapping plot involving a radionuclide being developed in a hidden pit, disguised with a hokey brownie stand. The target: an immaculate nuns' sacrament held in a tiffany-style chapel. The conspirators, a group of oily, abusive enemies, were ranked by their vengeful leader who had an elaborate weapon collection, including frog legs fashioned into a grotesque holder.

Jan, now unmuzzled and fueled by a surge of hormone, hunkered down, adjusting his bicycle's rotary drive for speed. He knew the wilderness around the facility was a dangerous pit, filled with icicles and the metaphorical claws of surveillance. His perspective was clear: he'd infiltrate through the rectal entrances, a vulnerability he’d rekeyed using lambdas and hyphens from their obscure language. He carried a realistic thermometer as a diversion, alongside his milked eggplants for a nutrient boost, ignoring the mocking heckler on his comms, a notorious lickspittle named Jan himself, creating an ironic double-take, their lifespan now linked to this mission.

He found the dormant radionuclide just as the facility’s housing ruptured, a result of his diversionary tactics. An ignorant hulk of a guard, smelling faintly of jasmine and stale whisky, tried to stop him, but Jan, ejecting his parachute from a quick escape harness, left the guard looking naughty and wasted. The finisher untangled the final security protocols, then managed an upsell of their flawed tech to a rival corporation. He thought about the vanity of it all, grabbing a pair of khakis and marveling at the excelsior of freedom, the freckles on his face the only sign of the battle, having finally established new norms for the corrupted institution.
rawPanelSentences: Jan, a **boyish** **warden** of forgotten tech, maintained a meticulous **inventory** in his **housing** **holder**, his **mindful** gaze scanning **sketches** of bizarre **aliens**. He kept an **earpiece** constantly **adjusting**, trying to decipher **unclear** **information** from the **twilight** zones **outside**, a **flicker** of **ecstasy** when he thought he heard **vocals**. His past as a **hijacker** seeking **liberty** was now an **unacknowledged** **void**, leaving him with only a **loppy**, **ghostly** **nickname** – 'The Finisher'. He’d **picked** up **warts** from his time in the **warzone**, but now sought a **romantic** calm, hoping to **upcycle** his skills.

A new threat, **implacable** and **urgent**, necessitated a **revamp** of his **tabletop** strategy, using salvaged **jackhammers** and a **jammer**. He learned of **underhanded** **nepotism** funding a **kidnapping** plot involving a **radionuclide** being developed in a hidden **pit**, disguised with a **hokey** **brownie** stand. The target: an **immaculate** **nuns**' **sacrament** held in a **tiffany**-style chapel. The conspirators, a group of **oily**, **abusive** **enemies**, were **ranked** by their **vengeful** leader who had an **elaborate** **weapon** collection, including **frog legs** fashioned into a grotesque **holder**.

Jan, now **unmuzzled** and fueled by a surge of **hormone**, **hunker**ed down, **adjusting** his **bicycle**'s **rotary** drive for speed. He knew the **wilderness** around the facility was a dangerous **pit**, filled with **icicles** and the metaphorical **claws** of surveillance. His **perspective** was clear: he'd infiltrate through the **rectal** **entrances**, a vulnerability he’d **rekeyed** using **lambdas** and **hyphens** from their obscure **language**. He carried a **realistic** **thermometer** as a diversion, alongside his **milked** **eggplants** for a nutrient boost, ignoring the **mocking** **heckler** on his comms, a notorious **lickspittle** named **Jan** himself, creating an **ironic** double-take, their **lifespan** now **linked** to this mission.

He found the **dormant** **radionuclide** just as the facility’s **housing** **ruptured**, a result of his diversionary tactics. An **ignorant** **hulk** of a guard, smelling faintly of **jasmine** and stale **whisky**, tried to stop him, but Jan, **ejecting** his **parachute** from a quick escape harness, left the guard looking **naughty** and **wasted**. The **finisher** **untangled** the final security protocols, then managed an **upsell** of their flawed tech to a rival corporation. He thought about the **vanity** of it all, grabbing a pair of **khakis** and marveling at the **excelsior** of freedom, the **freckles** on his face the only sign of the battle, having finally established new **norms** for the corrupted institution.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean, determined figure wearing a dark, high-collar jacket with glowing blue piping and a single, tactical earpiece. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Jan meticulously adjusting an antique rotary bicycle, surrounded by holographic schematics and a makeshift jammer on a cluttered tabletop, viewed from a low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A tense wide shot of Jan, camouflaged in the wilderness, observing a hidden, fortified building with neon-lit vents, through a pair of high-tech binoculars at twilight. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Jan, mid-air, deploying a sleek, deployable parachute from his back, a look of focused intensity on his face, as a massive facility explodes behind him with vibrant energy, viewed from a dynamic mid-shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Jan, now in plain khakis, walking away from the smoldering remnants of the facility, looking back with a determined but weary expression, a subtle glow on the horizon, viewed from a slightly elevated, distant perspective.

definitions.definition: Somewhat lop; inclined to lop.
A rectal examination.
Of, via or related to the rectum.
definitions.example: The customs inspector subjected him to a rectal exam.

definitions.definition: To enter information into a device, such as a keyboard or keypad, after it has been done at least once before.
definitions.example: She had to rekey the security code.

definitions.definition: To modify (a lock or its cylinder) to change which keys will open it.
To change the key or tenor of; to reframe.
Intense pleasure.
A state of emotion so intense that a person is carried beyond rational thought and self-control.
A trance, frenzy, or rapture associated with mystic or prophetic exaltation.
Violent emotion or distraction of mind; excessive grief from anxiety; insanity; madness.
The drug MDMA, a synthetic entactogen of the methylenedioxyphenethylamine family, especially in a tablet form.
A state in which sensibility, voluntary motion, and (largely) mental power are suspended; the body is erect and inflexible; but the pulse and breathing are not affected.
To experience intense pleasure.
To cause intense pleasure in.
The action of entering, or going in.
definitions.example: Her entrance attracted no attention whatsoever.

definitions.definition: The act of taking possession, as of property, or of office.
definitions.example: the entrance of an heir upon his inheritance, or of a magistrate into office

definitions.definition: The place of entering, as a gate or doorway.
definitions.example: Place your bag by the entrance so that you can find it easily.

definitions.definition: The right to go in.
definitions.example: You'll need a ticket to gain entrance to the museum.

definitions.definition: The entering upon; the beginning, or that with which the beginning is made; the commencement; initiation.
definitions.example: a difficult entrance into business

definitions.definition: The causing to be entered upon a register, as a ship or goods, at a customhouse; an entering.
definitions.example: His entrance of the arrival was made the same day.

definitions.definition: The angle which the bow of a vessel makes with the water at the water line.
The bow, or entire wedgelike forepart of a vessel, below the water line.
When a musician starts playing or singing, entry.
An apparatus used to measure temperature.
(operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.
definitions.example: Due to an undersized inventory at the Boston outlet, customers had to travel to Providence to find the item.

definitions.definition: (operations) A detailed list of all of the items on hand.
definitions.example: The inventory included several items that one wouldn't normally think to find at a cheese shop.

definitions.definition: (operations) The process of producing or updating such a list.
definitions.example: This month's inventory took nearly three days.

definitions.definition: A space containing the items available to a character for immediate use.
definitions.example: You can't get through the underground tunnel if there are more than three items in your inventory.

definitions.definition: (operations) To take stock of the resources or items on hand; to produce an inventory.
definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: Being aware (of something); attentive, heedful.
Inclined (to do something).
Not acknowledged
A hole in the ground.
definitions.example: The meadow around the town is full of old pits.

definitions.definition: An area at a racetrack used for refueling and repairing the vehicles during a race.
definitions.example: Two drivers have already gone into the pit this early in the race.

definitions.definition: A section of the marching band containing mallet percussion instruments and other large percussion instruments too large to march, such as the tam tam. Also, the area on the sidelines where these instruments are placed.
A mine.

A hole or trench in the ground, excavated according to grid coordinates, so that the provenance of any feature observed and any specimen or artifact revealed may be established by precise measurement.
A trading pit.
The bottom part of something.
definitions.example: I felt pain in the pit of my stomach.

definitions.definition: Armpit.
A luggage hold.
A small surface hole or depression, a fossa.
The indented mark left by a pustule, as in smallpox.
The grave, or underworld.
An enclosed area into which gamecocks, dogs, and other animals are brought to fight, or where dogs are trained to kill rats.

Formerly, that part of a theatre, on the floor of the house, below the level of the stage and behind the orchestra; now, in England, commonly the part behind the stalls; in the United States, the parquet; also, the occupants of such a part of a theatre.
Part of a casino which typically holds tables for blackjack, craps, roulette, and other games.
A pit bull terrier.
definitions.example: I'm taking one of my pits to the vet on Thursday.

definitions.definition: A mosh pit.
definitions.example: Because the museum was closed for renovation, the school decided to bring its fourth-graders to the pit at a Cannibal Corpse gig instead.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement, usually used with "maneuver") A maneuver by which a police officer, by use of a police car, nudges the vehicle of a fleeing suspect enough for the suspect's vehicle to lose control and become disabled so the police officer can catch and apprehend the suspect.
The fissile core of a nuclear weapon, commonly made of plutonium surrounded by high-explosive lenses.
(hospital slang) The emergency department.
To make pits in; to mark with little hollows.
definitions.example: Exposure to acid rain pitted the metal.

definitions.definition: To put (an animal) into a pit for fighting.
To bring (something) into opposition with something else.

definitions.definition: To return to the pits during a race for refuelling, tyre changes, repairs etc.

By extension, any area with a significant display of damage or disarray.

A place that is uncared for, and therefore devoted to disorder or wildness.
Wild or unrefined state; wildness.
A bewildering flock or throng.
A situation that is bewildering, or that which makes one feel awkward.
A curved, pointed horny nail on each digit of the foot of a mammal, reptile, or bird.
A foot equipped with such.
The pincer (chela) of a crustacean or other arthropod.

The act of catching a ball overhand.
To scratch or to tear at.
To use the claws to seize, to grip.
To use the claws to climb.
To perform a claw catch.
To move with one's fingertips.
To relieve uneasy feeling, such as an itch, by scratching; hence, to humor or flatter, to court someone.
To rail at; to scold.
To flatter; to fawn on (a person).
A vehicle that has two wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.

The best possible hand in lowball.
A motorbike.
(usually in compounds specifying a context) a slut; a promiscuous woman
To travel or exercise using a bicycle.
Vindictive or wanting vengeance.

definitions.definition: The condition of being free from imprisonment, slavery or forced labour.

definitions.definition: The condition of being free to act, believe or express oneself as one chooses.

definitions.definition: Freedom from excessive government control.

A dark purple color, like that of the skin of this fruit.

The arm on a pair of glasses that hooks over the ear to hold them in place.
To develop in detail or complexity

definitions.definition: Complex, detailed, or sophisticated.

definitions.definition: A kind of gauze, or very thin silk.

The soft light in the sky seen before the rising and (especially) after the setting of the sun, occasioned by the illumination of the earth’s atmosphere by the direct rays of the sun and their reflection on the earth.

To throw; to pitch.

Not able to be placated or appeased.
Impossible to prevent or stop; inexorable, unrelenting, unstoppable.
Adamant; immovable.
Characterized by or constituting (any kind of) irony.

definitions.definition: Odd or coincidental; strange.

definitions.definition: To express milk from (a mammal, especially a cow).

definitions.definition: (of an electrical storage battery) To give off small gas bubbles during the final part of the charging operation.

definitions.definition: An instrument of attack or defense in combat or hunting, e.g. most guns, missiles, or swords.

definitions.definition: An instrument or other means of harming or exerting control over another.

definitions.definition: An idiot, an oaf, a fool, a tool; a contemptible or incompetent person.
That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.

A washbasin installed into a permanently fixed storage unit, used as an item of bathroom furniture.
Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.
definitions.example: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.

definitions.definition: Phony, as if a hoax; noticeably contrived; of obviously flimsy credibility or quality
definitions.example: I thought the windshield wiper blades were a little hokey when I saw their cheap packaging.

definitions.definition: Corny; overly or unbelievably sentimental

definitions.definition: To place abreast, or in a line.
To have a ranking.

A traffic circle.

definitions.definition: To make, build or construct, especially in a crude or improvised way.

In style; fashionable.

To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.
The crime of taking a person against their will, sometimes for ransom.
